description: Crème brûlée is a rich custard dessert topped with a thin layer of hardened caramelized sugar. It is known for the contrast between the cool creamy custard and the crisp sugar crust.
preparation_steps: Prepare a custard mixture from cream, egg yolks, sugar, and flavoring.
Bake gently in ramekins, often in a water bath, until just set.
Sprinkle sugar on top and caramelize it with a torch or broiler just before serving.

health_advice.benefits: A small serving is often enough because of its richness.
health_advice.risks: High in sugar and saturated fat.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Keep portions small and treat it as an occasional dessert.

dietary_notes.note: Traditional crème brûlée is usually vegetarian and naturally gluten-free. Vegan versions are possible with plant-based custard ingredients.
